q: The basic principle of geometrical optics is based on:
exp: Geometrical optics assumes light travels in straight lines in a homogeneous medium.

q: The speed of light is maximum in:
exp: Light travels fastest in vacuum (c = 3 × 10^8 m/s).

q: The refractive index of a medium is defined as:
opts: Ratio of speed of light in medium to vacuum
Ratio of speed of light in vacuum to medium
exp: n = c/v, where c is vacuum speed and v is medium speed.

q: Snell’s law is:
opts: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
exp: Snell’s law relates angles of incidence and refraction.

q: When light travels from rarer to denser medium, it bends:
exp: Light slows down in denser medium, bending towards normal.

q: Critical angle occurs when:
exp: Critical angle is when refracted ray grazes along boundary.

q: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from:
opts: Denser to rarer medium with i > critical angle
exp: TIR occurs only when light goes from denser to rarer medium.

q: Which condition is necessary for total internal reflection?
exp: Incidence angle must exceed critical angle.

q: Lens formula is:
exp: Standard thin lens formula.

q: Power of lens is measured in:
exp: Power P = 1/f (in meters), unit is diopter.

q: If focal length increases, power of lens:
exp: P = 1/f, so inversely proportional.

q: A convex lens is also called:
exp: Convex lens converges parallel rays.

q: A concave lens always forms image that is:
exp: Concave lens forms virtual, erect, and smaller image.

q: Magnification of a lens is:
exp: Magnification m = v/u.

q: Mirror formula is:
exp: Same form as lens formula for spherical mirrors.

q: Focal length of plane mirror is:
exp: Plane mirror has infinite radius of curvature.

q: Image formed by plane mirror is:
exp: Plane mirror forms virtual upright image.

q: Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection in:
exp: Law of reflection states i = r.

q: Which mirror always forms virtual image?
exp: Convex and plane mirrors always form virtual images.

q: Refractive index increases when:
exp: n = c/v, so lower speed gives higher refractive index.

q: A ray passing through optical center of lens:
exp: Ray through optical center passes undeviated.

q: Critical angle depends on:
exp: sin C = 1/n.

q: If refractive index increases, critical angle:
exp: Higher n means smaller critical angle.

q: Image formed by convex mirror is always:
exp: Convex mirror always forms virtual diminished image.

q: If object is placed at center of curvature of concave mirror, image is:
exp: Image forms at same point with same size.

q: Speed of light in glass is approximately:
exp: Glass has refractive index ~1.5, so v ≈ c/1.5.

q: A convex lens forms real image when object is:
exp: Real image forms when object is beyond focal length.

q: Unit of refractive index is:
exp: It is a ratio of speeds, so dimensionless.

q: Which is correct about reflection?
exp: Reflection occurs in same medium.

q: If object distance equals image distance in mirror, mirror is:
exp: At C, object and image distances are equal.

q: Which phenomenon explains fiber optics?
exp: Optical fiber works on TIR principle.

q: A virtual image can be formed on:
opts: Both mirror and eye lens system
exp: Virtual images cannot be projected on screen but seen via optical systems.

q: In refraction, frequency of light:
exp: Frequency remains unchanged during refraction.

q: Which quantity changes in refraction?
exp: Speed and wavelength change, frequency remains constant.

q: A concave mirror forms real image when object is:
exp: Real image forms when object is beyond focal point.

q: Magnification of plane mirror is:
exp: Plane mirror produces same size image.

q: Which lens can form both real and virtual images?
exp: Convex lens forms real or virtual depending on object position.

q: A ray parallel to principal axis passes through:
exp: Parallel ray passes through focus after interaction with lens/mirror.

q: Which is correct?
exp: Concave lens spreads rays apart.

q: If object is between focus and lens (convex), image is:
exp: Object inside focal length gives virtual magnified image.

q: Which mirror has positive focal length?
exp: Convex mirror has positive focal length in sign convention.

q: Which law explains formation of image in pinhole camera?
exp: Light travels in straight lines forming inverted image.

q: If refractive index is 1, medium is:
exp: Vacuum has refractive index exactly 1.

q: Image formed by concave mirror at infinity occurs when object is:
exp: Object at infinity forms image at focus.

q: Which is correct about real image?
exp: Real images can be captured on screen.

q: Power of combination of lenses is:
exp: P_total = P1 + P2 + ...
